description_long: This object consists of a clustered arrangement of six pillar candles of varying heights, all with a creamy off-white wax exterior. Each candle features a visible, gently flickering flame at the top, with some showing pronounced melted wax drips running down the sides, adding to their realistic, used appearance. The tops of the candles are irregular and slightly concave, with some wicks and melted pools of wax visible around the flames. The candles are grouped closely together, forming a compact, unified display without any visible holder or base. The surface of the wax appears matte and slightly textured, with subtle variations in color and thickness. This arrangement is evocative of decorative candles often used for ambiance in urban outdoor or indoor settings, such as on a café table, park bench, or public event display.
description: A cluster of six off-white pillar candles of varying heights, each with a visible flame and wax drips, grouped together without a holder. The candles have irregular, melted tops and a realistic, matte wax texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six candles, with flames visible and prominent wax drips on the rightmost candles.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the staggered heights and the grouping, with more wax drips visible on the front candles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the candles, with less visible wax drips and a clearer view of the tallest candle's smooth surface.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the tallest candles, with fewer wax drips visible and a more uniform wax surface.
